About Me

I am passionate about technology because it allows people to create solutions and make daily life easier. My career goal is to become a software engineer and use AI and other algorithms to help others. To achieve this, I plan to complete all A-G requirements, strengthen my skills through advanced courses and pursue side projects which expand my knowledge.

My academic and extracurricular activities have helped me grow into a more focused student. As Captain of the Emerald High School Badminton team, I learned how to lead others and stay composed in challenging situations. My work in advanced math and computer science has made my communication and problem-solving skills better. These experiences have shown me the importance of the willingness to learn.

Through volunteering, tutoring and project-based work, I discovered my passion for helping others and taking initiative. These experiences helped me develop strong leadership skills and confidence in my ability to work with others. Thank you for taking the time to learn about me and I hope you explore the rest of my site!

Project 1: MIT App Inventor — Happy Accelerometer

Computer Science • Emerald High School • September 2025
Objective

Design an interactive puzzle app that challenges the user to keep a happy-face sprite centered on the screen by physically tilting their device. The app uses the phone's accelerometer sensor to detect motion and move the sprite accordingly.

My Responsibilities

Served as the coder for this project. I wrote all of the block-based logic in MIT App Inventor, including the accelerometer event handler, the sprite positioning formula, and the win/lose condition that detects when the sprite drifts too far from center.

What I Learned

I learned how apps track and update the coordinates of images on a screen in real time. I also gained an understanding of how hardware sensors like the accelerometer feed data into software, and how event-driven programming allows an app to respond instantly to user movement.

Skills Demonstrated

Block-based programming, sensor integration, coordinate-based logic, event-driven design, UI layout, problem-solving, and testing.

View Project Documentation →

Project 2: MIT App Inventor — Interview Questionnaire

Computer Science • Emerald High School • November 1 – November 3, 2025
Objective

Build an app that walks a user through a structured interview by displaying questions one at a time, recording spoken responses, and storing all answers for later review. The goal was to use technology to streamline the process of identifying real-world problems to solve through design thinking.

My Responsibilities

Served as the coder. I programmed the full question-sequencing logic so each question automatically advances after an answer is recorded. I also implemented the speech-to-text input, persistent data storage using TinyDB, a review screen listing all stored responses, and a reset function to clear previous interview data.

What I Learned

I learned how to manage application state across multiple steps using variables and lists. Working with TinyDB taught me how apps can store and retrieve data persistently between sessions. I also gained experience with speech recognition components and how to build a user flow that guides someone through a multi-step process automatically.

Skills Demonstrated

Block-based programming, list and variable management, persistent data storage, speech recognition integration, sequential UI logic, user experience design, and debugging.

View Project Documentation →
